relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: A severe misuse of sexuality is present in the community.
The deeper sickness is arrogance instead of mourning.
Paul judges the destructive pattern even while absent in body.
The assembly is asked to act in the name and power of Jesus.
The destructive flesh-pattern is exposed so the spirit may be saved/healed.
Boasting cannot coexist with corruption spreading through the lump.
Old leaven must be cleared for the community to live as new.
The feast is sincerity and truth, not harm and wickedness.
Paul clarifies earlier instruction about mixing with immorality.
He does not mean withdrawal from the world.
He means not normalizing destructive patterns inside siblinghood.
The community is responsible for its inner life.
Harm must be removed from the sacred body.

contemplative_note: 1 Corinthians 5 is not shame theology. It is community-boundary medicine: mourning, truth, and protection from patterns that corrupt the shared body.
